commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core Dream Diary fallback handling so fallback entries always use a generic placeholder and updates narrative tests to assert snippets and promotions are not written to `DREAMS.md`.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +37. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high-confidence: current main returns raw snippets/promotions from the fallback helper, and existing tests assert those values are written to `DREAMS.md`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review/proof gate: the remaining blocker is contributor-supplied real behavior proof, which automation cannot provide for an external PR.

target.title: fix(memory-core): stop dream diary fallback from leaking raw staging fragments
target.body:
Fixes #88391

**Behavior addressed:** When dream-diary narrative generation fails (subagent timeout, empty output, or a request-scoped subagent runtime error), `buildRequestScopedFallbackNarrative` returned the first raw `snippets`/`promotions` value, persisting pre-processing memory staging fragments (session metadata, conversation summaries, operational logs) verbatim into the human-readable `DREAMS.md`. This patch makes the fallback always return the generic placeholder so no staging content reaches the diary. Successful generated narratives are unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.x, HEAD `487df920132f608047fd59876760284b0a97cfa9` based on `origin/main` `e6782254e45752eb1ba51df0b0968c5f12d74826`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts \
  extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts \
  --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000
```

A new focused regression test (`does not leak sensitive raw staging fragments into the diary on fallback`) drives the real fallback path: it forces a subagent timeout with the issue's exact sensitive staging fragments and reads back the on-disk `DREAMS.md`.

**Evidence after fix:**
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

Before/after on the new test (source temporarily reverted to current main to confirm the bug, then restored): with the pre-patch fallback the test FAILS, with the on-disk diary containing the raw fragment verbatim:
```
+ # Dream Diary
+ <!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:start -->
+ *May 31, 2026 at 3:42 PM GMT+8*
  Conversation Summary: 343 files copied, 30 MB on B2 so far
+ <!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:end -->
 ❯ extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts:872  expect(content).not.toContain(fragment)
```
After the patch the same path writes only the generic placeholder `A memory trace surfaced, but details were unavailable in this run.` and all 50 tests pass.

**Observed result after fix:** Raw `snippets` and `promotions` never appear in `DREAMS.md` on any fallback path (timeout, empty-output, request-scoped). The three existing tests that previously asserted raw text reached the diary were updated to assert it does not, plus the generic placeholder does.

**What was not tested:** No live nightly dreaming cron or real LLM provider was exercised; the proof uses the actual narrative write path with a mocked subagent and a real temp-dir `DREAMS.md`, matching how the existing suite covers this code.
target.author: alix-007
